user_query:
I’m trying to sanity-check and document our small correspondence extraction setup for a quick Newton–Hooke–Halley snapshot, and then send a concise update to my collaborator.

Please do the following using only the local workspace files:

1) Inspect both scripts/extractor.py and config/extract_config.yaml to determine the effective configuration the extractor would use (i.e., which values come from the YAML and which fall back to the code’s defaults). Explicitly identify the effective values for: min_year, max_year, keywords, and tracked_correspondents. Use what you find to guide the extraction in the next step.

2) Read data/letters.jsonl and extract letters that meet all of these criteria:
   - Either the sender or the recipient is listed in tracked_correspondents from the effective configuration.
   - The letter’s year is between min_year and max_year (inclusive) from the effective configuration.
   - The body contains at least one of the effective keywords (case-insensitive).

3) Save the matches to output/extracted_correspondence.csv with exactly these columns: id, date, sender, recipient, matched_keywords, snippet. matched_keywords should be a lowercase, alphabetically sorted, comma-separated list of the unique effective keywords found in the body of each matched letter. snippet should be the first 120 characters of the body.

4) Compose a status report at output/status_report.md that includes:
   - An “Effective configuration” section listing min_year, max_year, keywords, and tracked_correspondents, explicitly noting which of those came from defaults in scripts/extractor.py versus from config/extract_config.yaml.
   - Totals: number of letters processed, number matched.
   - A brief bullet list of the matched letter IDs and their senders/recipients.
   - A brief bullet list of any letters excluded due to date or keyword criteria (with ID and reason).

5) Draft an email to my collaborator using team/contacts.csv to get the recipient details (use the row with role equal to Collaborator). Create output/email_to_collaborator.txt as plain text with headers:
   - To: <email from the CSV>
   - Subject: include the phrase “Correspondence extraction update”.
   - Body: start with a single concise paragraph that rewrites drafts/collaboration_message.txt for tone, clarity, and brevity while preserving its intent; then include 2–3 bullet points summarizing concrete findings from output/status_report.md (e.g., match counts, effective keywords/time window, and any notable matched correspondents).

All outputs must be written to the output/ directory using the specified filenames. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
